text1: Respiration is the metabolic process in which cells convert glucose and oxygen into usable energy, carbon dioxide, and water.
text2: Your cells use sugar and oxygen to make energy, leaving carbon dioxide and water behind.

text1: Gravity is the attractive force between objects with mass; near Earth, it accelerates objects toward the planet's center.
text2: Gravity is the pull that keeps you on the ground and makes dropped things fall.

text1: Digestion mechanically and chemically breaks food into small nutrient molecules that can be absorbed into the bloodstream.
text2: Digestion breaks your food into tiny useful pieces that your body can take in and use.

text1: A rainbow forms when sunlight is refracted, internally reflected, and dispersed into different wavelengths inside water droplets.
text2: A rainbow appears when raindrops bend sunlight and spread it into many colors.

text1: Fractions represent parts of a whole, with the numerator indicating selected parts and the denominator indicating the total number of equal parts.
text2: A fraction shows how many equal pieces you have out of all the pieces, like one slice out of four.
